channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: These men went to college in search of stable careers. They found it in nursing
item.description: Only about 1 in 8 nurses are men, but their share is growing. A strong job market for nurses and sustained efforts to recruit men into the field have made a difference, including in the South.
item.content:encoded:
Only about 1 in 8 nurses are men, but their share is growing. A strong job market for nurses and sustained efforts to recruit men into the field have made a difference, including in the South.

(Image credit: Andi Rice for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: FDA still focused on Taylor Farms lettuce as Cyclospora source despite retracted test
item.description: The FDA retracted its only positive test for cyclospora, the parasite at the center of a multistate outbreak, on Taylor Farms lettuce. But the agency believes lettuce from the brand is still linked.
item.content:encoded: The FDA retracted its only positive test for cyclospora, the parasite at the center of a multistate outbreak, on Taylor Farms lettuce. But the agency believes lettuce from the brand is still linked.
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi

item.title: Trump cuts to advisory groups leaves hospitals without key expertise on disease
item.description: Under the Trump administration, dozens of advisory committees have been ended or frozen, including one on controlling infection in healthcare settings.
item.content:encoded: Under the Trump administration, dozens of advisory committees have been ended or frozen, including one on controlling infection in healthcare settings.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: FDA still focused on Taylor Farms lettuce as Cyclospora source despite retracted test
item.description: The FDA says lettuce distributed by Taylor Fresh Foods and Taylor Farms de Mexico is still linked to the multistate outbreak of cyclosporiasis. But the source may be difficult to definitively trace.
item.content:encoded: The FDA says lettuce distributed by Taylor Fresh Foods and Taylor Farms de Mexico is still linked to the multistate outbreak of cyclosporiasis. But the source may be difficult to definitively trace.
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi

item.title: FDA retracts positive cyclospora test on Taylor Farms lettuce
item.description: The FDA says the test identifying cyclospora in Taylor Farms iceberg lettuce was a "false positive." But the lettuce is still linked epidemiologically to cases of the illness in a multistate outbreak.
item.content:encoded: The FDA says the test identifying cyclospora in Taylor Farms iceberg lettuce was a "false positive." But the lettuce is still linked epidemiologically to cases of the illness in a multistate outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi

item.title: At least 930 people have died in Congo's Ebola outbreak
item.description: Congo's health ministry said the deaths were recorded out of the 2,344 cases confirmed as of Saturday. Thirty-seven new deaths were recorded in the previous 24 hours in one of the highest totals.
item.content:encoded:
Congo's health ministry said the deaths were recorded out of the 2,344 cases confirmed as of Saturday. Thirty-seven new deaths were recorded in the previous 24 hours in one of the highest totals.

(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Deloitte-run systems denied Medicaid to disabled people. New laws could make it worse
item.description: Complex computer systems that decide whether people get Medicaid benefits have a history of errors. Now, changes to comply with Trump's tax and domestic policy law are inundating those systems.
item.content:encoded:
Complex computer systems that decide whether people get Medicaid benefits have a history of errors. Now, changes to comply with Trump's tax and domestic policy law are inundating those systems.

(Image credit: Kate Wells for KFF Health News)
item.dc:creator: Rachana Pradhan

item.title: So distressed you can't think straight? Lower the intensity with these 4 skills
item.description: A clinical psychologist shares effective strategies to quickly de-escalate stress and anxiety. That includes dunking your head into a bowl of ice water to trigger your mammalian dive reflex.
item.content:encoded:
A clinical psychologist shares effective strategies to quickly de-escalate stress and anxiety. That includes dunking your head into a bowl of ice water to trigger your mammalian dive reflex.

(Image credit: Jesse Finkelstein/Sonya Barlas/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: New study finds avocado oil products may contain cheaper oils
item.description: Sales of avocado oil and products made with it are booming as consumers willingly pay a premium, drawn by the oil's health halo. New research finds many products actually contain other, cheaper oils.
item.content:encoded: Sales of avocado oil and products made with it are booming as consumers willingly pay a premium, drawn by the oil's health halo. New research finds many products actually contain other, cheaper oils.
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: Violence repeatedly erupts in dementia care despite warnings, inspections show
item.description: Nursing homes and assisted living facilities can be fertile ground for violence between residents with dementia, including fatal assaults. Court records and inspecitions how safeguards fall short.
item.content:encoded:
Nursing homes and assisted living facilities can be fertile ground for violence between residents with dementia, including fatal assaults. Court records and inspecitions how safeguards fall short.

(Image credit: Marco Cecchetto)
item.dc:creator: Jordan Rau
